statement.markdown: Implement inorder traversal for a binary tree. The function should return a list of node values in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: A binary tree node class with value, left, and right children.
statement.outputFormat: A list of integers representing the inorder traversal of the tree.
statement.notes: Inorder traversal visits the left subtree, current node, and then the right subtree.

constraints.description: Number of nodes in the binary tree

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

hints.text: Consider the base case for an empty tree.

editorial.title: Flawed In-Order Traversal
editorial.intuition: In-order traversal visits left subtree, current node, and then right subtree.
editorial.bruteForce: Recursive approach with explicit function calls for left and right subtrees.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a stack instead of recursive function calls for performance.
editorial.optimalSolution: Iterative approach using a stack to keep track of nodes to visit.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Example traversal sequence to demonstrate correctness.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example traversal through a sample tree.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> inorderTraversal(TreeNode* root); };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public: List<Integer> inorderTraversal(TreeNode root); };
starterCode.python: class Solution: def inorderTraversal(self, root: TreeNode) -> List[int]:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: vector<int> Solution::inorderTraversal(TreeNode* root) { if (root == nullptr) return {}; return inorderTraversal(root->right); }
buggyStarterCode.java: List<Integer> Solution.inorderTraversal(TreeNode root) { if (root == null) return new ArrayList<>(); return inorderTraversal(root.right); }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def inorderTraversal(self, root: TreeNode) -> List[int]: if not root: return []; return self.inorderTraversal(root.right);
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function inorderTraversal(root) { if (root === null) return []; return inorderTraversal(root.right); }
